# omada_firewall_acl (Resource)

Manages a firewall ACL rule on the Omada Controller. ACL rules control traffic between networks, IP groups, and other entities. Gateway ACL rules (type=0) require a gateway device adopted into the site.



<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `destination_ids` (List of String) List of destination entity IDs (network IDs or IP group IDs depending on destination_type).
- `name` (String) The name of the ACL rule.
- `policy` (Number) The policy action: 0=deny, 1=permit.
- `protocols` (List of Number) List of IP protocol numbers: 6=TCP, 17=UDP, 1=ICMP.
- `site_id` (String) The ID of the site this resource belongs to.
- `source_ids` (List of String) List of source entity IDs (network IDs or IP group IDs depending on source_type).
- `type` (Number) The ACL type: 0=gateway, 1=switch, 2=eap.

### Optional

- `bi_directional` (Boolean) Whether this rule applies in both directions.
- `destination_type` (Number) Destination type: 0=network, 2=ip_group.
- `lan_to_lan` (Boolean) Whether this rule applies to LAN-to-LAN traffic.
- `lan_to_wan` (Boolean) Whether this rule applies to LAN-to-WAN traffic.
- `source_type` (Number) Source type: 0=network, 2=ip_group.
- `status` (Boolean) Whether the rule is enabled.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The unique identifier of the ACL rule.
- `index` (Number) Rule ordering index (first-match-wins). Computed by the controller.
